sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Hedge Funds View Inspire Medical (INSP) as One of the Best Small-Cap Healthcare Stocks
feed.summary: Hedge funds reportedly consider Inspire Medical Systems Inc. (NYSE: INSP) among the top small-cap healthcare stocks to buy. The company, which specializes in treatments for obstructive sleep apnea (OSA), recently received positive news regarding CMS reimbursement and new HCPCS codes, which analysts view as directionally positive despite some remaining uncertainties. Inspire Medical develops and markets neurostimulation technology for OSA treatment.

feed.title: Insider Sell Alert: Hon Lee Sells Shares of Aurora Mobile Ltd (J
feed.summary: Hon Lee, a Director at Aurora Mobile Ltd (JG), sold 100 shares of the company on March 30, 2026, reducing his total holdings to 16,746 shares. Over the past year, Lee has sold a total of 1,552 shares with no purchases, and there have been no insider buys and two insider sells for the company. Aurora Mobile Ltd, trading at $7 per share with a market cap of $41.730 million, is considered fairly valued with a price-to-GF-Value ratio of 0.97.

feed.title: Here Are Wednesday’s Top Wall Street Analyst Research Calls: Boeing, Datadog, Disney, Formula One Group, Nike, Rivian, Rocket Lab, ServiceNow, and More
feed.summary: This article compiles top Wall Street analyst research calls from Wednesday, April 1, 2026, including upgrades for Formula One Group, Rivian, United Health Group, Disney, and Wells Fargo. Downgrades include Apellis Pharmaceutical, Centessa Pharmaceuticals, First Citizens Bancshares, Nike, and PPG Industries, while Boeing, Datadog, Northrop Grumman, Rocket Lab, and ServiceNow received new "Buy" or "Overweight" initiations. The report also provides a market overview, noting a rally driven by hopes for an end to the Iran war and significant movements in various asset classes like stocks, bonds, oil, gold, and cryptocurrency.

feed.title: Clorox Completes Acquisition of GOJO Industries, Makers of Purell®
feed.summary: The Clorox Company has finalized its acquisition of GOJO Industries, the makers of the Purell brand, thus expanding Clorox's product portfolio in health and hygiene solutions. This strategic move unites two companies committed to a cleaner and healthier world, leveraging their combined expertise in consumer branding and B2B capabilities. The GOJO business will now operate as Clorox Purell and continue to be headquartered in Akron, Ohio.

feed.title: Janux Therapeutics Announces Development Candidate Nomination Under Bristol Myers Squibb Collaboration, Triggering $35 Million Milestone Payment
feed.summary: Janux Therapeutics has nominated a development candidate under its collaboration with Bristol Myers Squibb, triggering a $35 million milestone payment. This candidate, developed using Janux's TRACTr platform, targets an undisclosed solid tumor antigen. The milestone highlights Janux's strategy of internal innovation and strategic partnerships, with Bristol Myers Squibb responsible for clinical development and commercialization after IND submission.

feed.title: Wells Fargo Adjusts Price Target on Lennox International to $480 From $560, Maintains Equalweight Rating
feed.summary: Wells Fargo has lowered its price target for Lennox International (LII) from $560 to $480 while keeping an Equalweight rating on the stock. This adjustment reflects a revised valuation perspective for the HVACR solutions provider. The article also notes other recent analyst adjustments and company news, including Lennox's dividend declaration and strategic moves into residential water heating.

feed.title: Janux Therapeutics Announces Development Candidate Nomination Under Bristol Myers Squibb Collaboration, Triggering $35 Million Milestone Payment
feed.summary: Janux Therapeutics announced the nomination of a development candidate under its collaboration agreement with Bristol Myers Squibb, triggering a $35 million milestone payment. This candidate utilizes Janux's TRACTr platform to target an undisclosed solid tumor antigen, marking a significant step towards IND-enabling studies and clinical development. Janux will continue preclinical development, with Bristol Myers Squibb responsible for clinical development and commercialization, and Janux eligible for further milestone payments and royalties.

feed.title: Vertex wins FDA nod to expand cystic fibrosis drug indications By Investing.com
feed.summary: Vertex Pharmaceuticals announced that the FDA has expanded the indications for its cystic fibrosis drugs, ALYFTREK and TRIKAFTA, to include more patients. This expansion covers approximately 800 additional people in the U.S. and means about 95% of cystic fibrosis patients in the U.S. are now eligible for CFTR modulator treatment. Despite recent downward revisions in earnings estimates by some analysts, Vertex maintains a strong market position and profitability.

feed.title: Burrito Day 2026 deals: Score free food from Chipotle, Qdoba, El Pollo Loco, and more
feed.summary: National Burrito Day on April 2, 2026, brings numerous deals for free burritos and BOGO offers from popular chains. Chipotle, Qdoba, El Pollo Loco, Baja Fresh, Chronic Tacos, Moe's Southwest Grill, and Tortilla are all participating with special promotions for rewards members and in-store purchases. These deals allow customers to enjoy discounted or free burritos, some even offering a chance to win free burritos for a year.

feed.title: Metro by T-Mobile announces bold new direction as smartphone price anxiety takes hold
feed.summary: Facing "smartphone price anxiety," Metro by T-Mobile launched a new fragrance called "CALLoGNE" to evoke the smell of a new phone. While initially presented as a real product, it was revealed to be an April Fools' prank. Metro by T-Mobile pivoted to promoting deals on affordable handsets to give customers the "new-phone feeling" for real.

feed.title: Agilent Announces the BioTek Cytation 9 Cell Imaging Multimode Reader
feed.summary: Agilent Technologies, Inc. announced the launch of the BioTek Cytation 9 cell imaging multimode reader, which integrates multimode microplate reading and high-content cell imaging into a single platform. This new instrument builds on a decade of Cytation innovation, offering expanded imaging capabilities and advanced hybrid microplate detection for faster workflows in basic research and high-content screening. Designed for both cell-based and biochemical assays, the Cytation 9 aims to accelerate discoveries in cell biology and drug development, particularly for pharmaceutical and biotechnology laboratories.

feed.title: TE Connectivity sets April 22 earnings release, 8:30 a.m. call
feed.summary: TE Connectivity (NYSE: TEL) will report its second-quarter fiscal 2026 results before market open on April 22, 2026, followed by an investor conference call at 8:30 a.m. ET. Investors can access the call via the company's website or by telephone, with a replay available later that day. This announcement aligns with TE Connectivity's consistent pattern of providing clear pre-earnings communication.

feed.title: Before markets open, Thermo Fisher posts Q1 results on April 23
feed.summary: Thermo Fisher Scientific Inc. (NYSE: TMO) announced it will release its first-quarter 2026 financial results before market open on Thursday, April 23, 2026. The company will hold a conference call at 8:30 a.m. ET on the same day to discuss its financial performance and future expectations. The call will be webcast live on the "Investors" section of their website, www.thermofisher.com.

feed.title: Halliburton buys Sekal as drilling software cuts well times up to 25%
feed.summary: Halliburton (NYSE: HAL) has acquired Sekal AS from Sumitomo Corporation (TYO: 8053), a move aimed at enhancing Halliburton's drilling automation services. This acquisition integrates Sekal’s DrillTronics® automation platform with Halliburton's LOGIX® automation, promising to lower well construction costs, increase recovery, and reduce operational risks. The combined technology has already demonstrated a reduction of up to 25% in well delivery times through precise well placement and dynamic drilling parameter optimization.

feed.title: Teledyne Strengthens Commitment to the Space Sector
feed.summary: Teledyne Technologies Incorporated has announced the integration of its space-focused technologies and businesses, forming "Teledyne Space" to reinforce its long-term commitment to the global space sector. This new collective aims to align expertise in sensing, electronics, components, and mission-critical operations to meet the growing demand for advanced space technologies. Teledyne Space will showcase its capabilities, including imaging detectors, microwave devices, and radiation-tolerant semiconductors, at the 2026 Space Symposium in Colorado Springs.

feed.title: Stryker to announce financial results for its first quarter of fiscal year 2026
feed.summary: Stryker (NYSE: SYK) will report its first-quarter fiscal year 2026 financial results on Thursday, April 30, 2026. A press release will be issued around 4:05 p.m. ET, followed by a webcast at 4:30 p.m. ET to discuss the results. The financial information will cover the quarter ending March 31, 2026.

feed.title: Sherwin-Williams to give Q2 and 2026 outlook on April 28 call
feed.summary: Sherwin-Williams (NYSE: SHW) will announce its first-quarter 2026 financial results before market open on Tuesday, April 28, 2026. The company will hold a conference call at 10:00 a.m. EDT on the same day to discuss Q1 results and provide outlook for the second quarter and full-year 2026. The call will feature Chair, President, and CEO Heidi Petz along with other senior executives, and a webcast will be available on the company's website.

feed.title: AllianzIM Expands Buffered ETF Suite with Latest International Fund and Seven New Quarterly-Reset Strategies
feed.summary: Allianz Investment Management LLC (AllianzIM) has expanded its Buffered ETF lineup with eight new strategies, including a new international Uncapped Buffered ETF and seven quarterly-reset, single-ticker buffered ETFs. These new offerings provide investors with more options for managing downside risk and gaining exposure to various U.S. and global indexes. The funds feature defined downside buffers and upside participation levels, leveraging AllianzIM's expertise in risk management and in-house hedging capabilities.

feed.title: Alphabet Inc. Class C Stock: Core Business Drivers, Investor Relevance, and Key Risks in the Evolving Tech Landscape
feed.summary: Alphabet Inc. Class C stock (GOOG) offers North American investors exposure to a diversified tech empire encompassing digital advertising, cloud computing, and AI innovations. Led by Google Search and YouTube, with Google Cloud as a significant growth area, the company maintains dominant market positions, although it faces ongoing regulatory scrutiny and competitive pressures. Investors should monitor its AI advancements, capital return policies, and the outcomes of antitrust cases for long-term portfolio management.

feed.title: Executive pay, dividends and board votes at ONEOK (NYSE: OKE)
feed.summary: ONEOK, Inc. (NYSE: OKE) is holding its virtual annual meeting on May 20, 2026, where shareholders will vote on the election of ten directors, the ratification of PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP as auditor, and an advisory approval of executive compensation. The company reported strong financial performance in 2025 with $5.7 billion in consolidated operating income and $3.5 billion in net income, both up from 2024, and increased its dividends to $4.12 per share in 2025 and $1.07 per share quarterly in February 2026. Executive compensation features a significant "at-risk" component tied to financial and operational performance, with CEO Pierce H. Norton II receiving $12.6 million in total direct compensation for 2025.

feed.title: The Quest for Energy Independence - Northern Trust - Commentaries
feed.summary: The Iran war has severely disrupted global energy supplies, particularly crude oil and LNG, leading to a new urgency for energy independence worldwide. Nations like Japan, India, and European countries are exploring diverse strategies, including restarting nuclear plants, increasing coal use, and investing in new renewable and traditional energy sources, despite potential environmental trade-offs. The conflict highlights the risks of overreliance on Middle Eastern energy and the global push towards more secure, albeit potentially more carbon-intensive, energy mixes.

feed.title: Coca-Cola plans to invest $1 billion in South Africa through 2030
feed.summary: Coca-Cola and its South African bottlers plan to invest $1.05 billion (17.6 billion rand) in South Africa by 2030. This investment aims to expand production capacity, strengthen distribution, and accelerate innovation within the "Coca-Cola system's" value chain. The announcement was made by Luis Felipe Avellar at an investment conference in Johannesburg, where President Cyril Ramaphosa is seeking new investments.

feed.title: West Pharmaceutical Services opens expanded Dublin facility for injectable therapies
feed.summary: West Pharmaceutical Services announced the opening of an expanded 165,000-square-foot facility for contract services in Dublin, Ireland, creating 330 new jobs. This expansion enhances West's capabilities in high-volume injectable therapies, including GLP-1 treatments for diabetes and obesity, and increases global capacity for integrated drug delivery solutions. The company's comprehensive contract services, West Vantage, aim to accelerate market entry for pharmaceutical and biotechnology partners by providing scalable solutions for life-enhancing therapies.

feed.title: Alphabet Inc. $GOOGL Shares Bought by Well Done LLC
feed.summary: Well Done LLC significantly increased its stake in Alphabet Inc. (NASDAQ:GOOGL) by 93.3% in the fourth quarter, purchasing an additional 2,194 shares, bringing their total to 4,546 shares valued at approximately $1.423 million. Alphabet exceeded quarterly earnings expectations, reporting $2.82 EPS and $113.83 billion in revenue, and holds a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating from analysts with a target price around $368. Recent positive catalysts include the launch of a cheaper AI video model and a reiterated "Buy" rating with a $400 price target from Needham, though the company faces regulatory scrutiny and partner headwinds.

feed.title: Hengehold Capital Management LLC Acquires 19,308 Shares of Procter & Gamble Company (The) $PG
feed.summary: Hengehold Capital Management LLC increased its stake in Procter & Gamble (NYSE:PG) by 4.7% in the fourth quarter of 2025, adding 19,308 shares to hold a total of 426,481 shares valued at $61.12 million. This move makes P&G its second-largest holding, representing 5.1% of its portfolio. Despite institutional buying, insider selling and analyst downgrades due to input-cost pressures suggest potential near-term headwinds for the company.

feed.title: YMAX ETF Holdings List — AMEX:YMAX
feed.summary: This article provides a detailed list of the YMAX ETF holdings, also known as the YieldMax Universe Fund of Option Income ETFs, as of February 27, 2026. It includes the weight, number of shares, and market value for each holding, such as the U.S. Dollar and various YieldMax Option Income Strategy ETFs targeting companies like CRCL, NFLX, COIN, SNOW, RBLX, DKNG, HOOD, and PLTR.

feed.title: Wealth Enhancement Advisory Services LLC Reduces Stake in MGM Resorts International $MGM
feed.summary: Wealth Enhancement Advisory Services LLC decreased its holdings in MGM Resorts International by 33.3% in Q4, selling 42,950 shares, though institutional investors still hold a significant portion of the company. Notably, IAC Inc. increased its stake by purchasing 450,000 shares, while director Keith A. Meister sold 37,500 shares. MGM currently holds an average "Hold" rating from analysts with an average price target of $41.94, and recently reported quarterly EPS of $1.60 on revenues of $4.61 billion, beating analyst estimates.

feed.title: Zimmer Biomet Holdings, Inc. $ZBH Shares Sold by Wealth Enhancement Advisory Services LLC
feed.summary: Wealth Enhancement Advisory Services LLC reduced its stake in Zimmer Biomet Holdings, Inc. by 31.5% in the fourth quarter, selling 15,033 shares. Despite this, Zimmer Biomet reported strong Q results, topping revenue and EPS estimates, and declared a quarterly dividend. Other institutional investors, however, increased their holdings in the medical equipment provider.

feed.title: A Look At Delta Air Lines (DAL) Valuation After Amazon Project Kuiper Wi Fi Partnership Announcement
feed.summary: Delta Air Lines (DAL) recently announced a multi-year partnership with Amazon's Project Kuiper to equip 500 aircraft with satellite Wi-Fi starting in 2028, leading to a 5.21% share price jump. While the stock has seen significant returns over the past year and three years, its valuation presents a mixed picture. The most popular narrative suggests Delta is slightly overvalued at $66.48 with a fair value of $63.21, primarily due to strong unit economics, but a discounted cash flow (DCF) model indicates a 48.6% discount to an estimated $129.29 value, suggesting deep value.

feed.title: MGM's Luxor buffet closes as Strip dining trends continue to evolve
feed.summary: MGM Resorts International has closed The Buffet at Luxor, further reducing the number of all-you-can-eat options on the Las Vegas Strip. This closure reflects a continuing trend of casinos moving away from buffet formats, especially post-COVID-19, due to public health concerns and changing consumer behavior. Despite ongoing contraction on the Strip, some off-Strip properties still offer buffet dining, suggesting demand persists in certain market segments.

feed.title: The Hershey Company Stock: Reaffirming 2026 Growth Targets Amid Strategic Shift to Next-Gen Snacking
feed.summary: The Hershey Company reaffirmed its 2026 net sales growth outlook of 4% to 5% and organic sales growth of 2.5% to 3.5% at its recent Investor Day, signaling a strategic shift towards next-generation snacking beyond traditional chocolate. Despite an initial 3% dip in stock price post-event, the company aims for margin recovery by 2027 and double-digit EPS growth long-term, driven by acquisitions like SkinnyPop and Dot's Pretzels. While facing challenges from commodity costs and increased competition, Hershey maintains a strong financial position and defensive market appeal for investors.

feed.title: MiNK Therapeutics Announces Abstract Acceptance for Presentation at the American Thoracic Society (ATS) 2026 International Conference
feed.summary: MiNK Therapeutics announced that an abstract on its investigational iNKT cell therapy, agenT-797, has been accepted for presentation at the American Thoracic Society (ATS) International Conference in May 2026. The abstract details a combination immunotherapy approach for persistent Coccidioides immitis infection. The presentation will feature Dr. Terese Hammond and will occur on May 20, 2026, though specific data will not be disclosed until the conference.

feed.title: Earnings Flash (CAG) Conagra Brands, Inc. Posts Q3 Adjusted EPS $0.39 per Share, vs. FactSet Est of $0.40
feed.summary: Conagra Brands, Inc. reported its Q3 adjusted EPS at $0.39 per share, falling short of the FactSet estimate of $0.40. The company also announced an updated fiscal 2026 adjusted EPS outlook and reported Q3 revenue of $2.79 billion, which slightly exceeded the FactSet estimate of $2.76 billion. Conagra Brands expects weak annual profit due to rising costs.

feed.title: Conagra (NYSE:CAG) Posts Better-Than-Expected Sales In Q1 CY2026
feed.summary: Conagra Brands (CAG) reported Q1 CY2026 results, with sales of $2.79 billion beating analyst expectations by 1.1% despite a 1.9% year-on-year decline. The company's adjusted EPS of $0.39 missed analyst estimates by 2.6%. Management reiterated its full-year Adjusted EPS guidance of $1.78 at the midpoint, while sales volumes were flat year-on-year.

feed.title: Bernstein reiterates Costco stock rating on membership strength
feed.summary: Bernstein SocGen Group reiterated an Outperform rating and a $1,170.00 price target on Costco Wholesale (NASDAQ:COST), citing the company's strong membership-based business model and underappreciated international growth potential. The firm estimates Costco could expand to over 2,300 stores globally, a growth runway of more than 50 years, which is expected to support a 7% compound annual growth in membership fee income. These insights are supported by Costco's robust financials, product diversification efforts, and positive analyst revisions.

feed.title: Telsey cuts J.Jill stock price target on margin pressure, tariffs
feed.summary: Telsey Advisory Group has lowered its price target for J.Jill Inc. (NYSE:JILL) to $13 from $16, citing anticipated topline and margin pressures in the company's fiscal 2026 outlook. Despite a strong fourth quarter performance and solid gross profit margins, the women's apparel retailer faces challenges from ongoing tariffs and a strategic investment phase aimed at expanding its customer base. Other analysts like TD Cowen, BTIG, and Jefferies have also adjusted their price targets, reflecting cautious optimism about J.Jill's future amidst market uncertainties.

feed.title: Intrusion Inc. (INTZ) stock sinks as market gains: Here's why
feed.summary: This article reports on Intrusion Inc. (INTZ) stock experiencing a decline while the overall market saw gains. The content currently available is incomplete, making it difficult to ascertain the specific reasons behind INTZ's underperformance.

feed.title: Goldman Sachs Initiates Coverage of Immunome (IMNM) with Buy Recommendation
feed.summary: Goldman Sachs has initiated coverage on Immunome (IMNM) with a Buy recommendation. This move suggests a positive outlook from the investment bank regarding Immunome's future performance and potential as an investment. No further details about the rating or price target were provided in this snippet.

feed.title: Heritage Commerce Corp (NASDAQ:HTBK) Short Interest Up 19.2% in March
feed.summary: Heritage Commerce Corp (NASDAQ:HTBK) experienced a 19.2% increase in short interest during March, reaching 1,971,456 shares, which represents about 3.3% of its float. The company recently reported strong Q4 results, beating EPS and revenue estimates, and declared a quarterly dividend of $0.13. Analyst ratings for HTBK currently suggest a "Moderate Buy" consensus with a price target of $12.25.

feed.title: Does Prologis’ US$1.6 Billion GIC JV Reframe Its Asset-Light Strategy For Long-Term Investors (PLD)?
feed.summary: Prologis and GIC have formed a US$1.6 billion joint venture for build-to-suit logistics facilities in the U.S., deepening Prologis's role as an asset manager. This partnership reinforces the strategic capital model by aligning development expertise with institutional investment and demand-driven projects. The move, coupled with a dividend increase, suggests confidence in recurring cash flows, though elevated market vacancy and cautious tenant demand remain potential risks for long-term investors.

feed.title: Franklin Resources Inc Stock: Global Asset Manager Positions for Growth Amid Evolving Markets
feed.summary: Franklin Resources Inc (NYSE: BEN), operating as Franklin Templeton, manages $1.74 trillion in assets and is positioning for growth through strategic leadership changes and diversified offerings. The company provides a wide range of investment products including mutual funds, ETFs, and closed-end funds, catering to both retail and institutional clients globally. Despite intense competition and fee pressures in the asset management sector, Franklin's scale, multi-brand heritage, and focus on active management aim to ensure long-term stability and attract North American investors.

feed.title: Conagra Brands expects weak annual profit on rising costs
feed.summary: Conagra Brands is forecasting its annual profit at the low end of its guidance range due to elevated costs and a volatile macroeconomic environment. Consumers are cutting back on spending and opting for cheaper brands, further impacting sales, alongside a shift towards healthier foods due to the rising adoption of weight-loss drugs. The company expects annual adjusted profit per share of $1.70 and annual net sales at the midpoint of its previous forecast.

feed.title: The Bull Case For Microchip Technology (MCHP) Could Change Following New Automotive SiP And mSiC Launches - Learn Why
feed.summary: Microchip Technology recently launched new automotive SiP and mSiC modules, targeting vehicle electronics and high-performance power systems with simplified design. While these products align with Microchip's long-term strategy in auto and power, they do not immediately alter the company's focus on managing elevated inventory and debt. Investors should watch how these new product ramps might eventually help reduce inventories and improve margins, even though the timing and scale of this impact remain uncertain.

feed.title: Hut 8 (HUT) Is Down 11.9% After $7 Billion AI Lease Shift From Pure Bitcoin Mining
feed.summary: Hut 8 Corp. is transitioning from a pure Bitcoin mining company to a broader digital infrastructure firm, evidenced by a new 15-year, US$7.0 billion AI data center lease. This strategic shift aims to stabilize cash flows by combining Bitcoin mining with contracted AI compute and energy assets, though execution risk and Bitcoin price volatility remain significant factors for investors. The article highlights both the potential for substantial revenue and earnings growth by 2028 and the wide range of analyst opinions on the company's long-term profitability.

feed.title: Kevlar and Nomex change hands in DuPont's $1.8 billion sale
feed.summary: DuPont has finalized the sale of its Aramids business, which includes the Kevlar and Nomex brands, to Arclin for a total value of approximately $1.8 billion. The transaction provides DuPont with $1.2 billion in pre-tax cash, a $300 million note receivable, and a 16% non-controlling equity stake in Arclin valued at $325 million. The results of the Aramids business have been retrospectively reclassified as discontinued operations starting from the third quarter of 2025.

feed.title: DuPont Completes Divestiture of Aramids Business
feed.summary: DuPont has completed the divestiture of its Aramids business (Kevlar® and Nomex®) to Arclin, a portfolio company of TJC, L.P. The transaction is valued at approximately $1.8 billion, with DuPont receiving cash proceeds, a note receivable, and a non-controlling equity interest in Arclin. This move reclassifies the Aramids business's financial results as discontinued operations from the third quarter of 2025.

feed.title: U.S. Bank taking over Amazon's small-business card program from American Express
feed.summary: U.S. Bancorp is acquiring Amazon's small-business credit card program from American Express, partnering with Mastercard for the co-branded cards. This move positions U.S. Bank, the largest bank in Southeast Wisconsin by deposit market share, to manage a widely used financial product.

feed.title: MiNK to present severe infection therapy study in Orlando in May
feed.summary: MiNK Therapeutics (NASDAQ: INKT) announced the acceptance of an abstract for presentation at the American Thoracic Society (ATS) 2026 International Conference in Orlando, scheduled for May 15-20, 2026. The presentation will detail a combination of N-803 and agenT-797 as an investigational therapy for unresolving Coccidioides immitis infection. No data or results will be disclosed before the conference presentation on May 20, 2026.

feed.title: A two-drug small cell lung cancer trial pairs Amgen with Zai Lab
feed.summary: Zai Lab and Amgen have initiated a global Phase 1b clinical trial to evaluate a novel two-drug combination for extensive-stage small cell lung cancer (ES-SCLC). The study will combine Zai Lab’s DLL3-targeting antibody-drug conjugate (ADC), zocilurtatug pelitecan (zoci), with Amgen’s DLL3-targeting Bispecific T-cell Engager (BiTE®) therapy, IMDELLTRA®. This collaboration aims to enhance treatment efficacy by targeting DLL3, a protein overexpressed in ES-SCLC, through complementary mechanisms.

feed.title: FDA move makes 800 more US patients eligible for Vertex CF drugs
feed.summary: Vertex Pharmaceuticals (VRTX) announced FDA approval for expanded use of its cystic fibrosis drugs, ALYFTREK and TRIKAFTA. This label extension makes these treatments available to approximately 800 additional U.S. patients, covering about 95% of all people with CF in the country. The expansion includes any CFTR gene variant that results in the production of the CFTR protein, underscoring the drugs' broad impact regardless of the variant's location.
